etymology.description: 雨(雨) ('rain') + 申(申) ('lightning') = depiction of a thunderstorm -> electric
etymology.interpretation: The character 電(電) combines 雨(雨) (yǔ, 'rain') as the semantic element suggesting a natural event involving water, with 申(申) (shēn, 'lightning') indicating the associated sound and visual phenomenon of a thunderstorm, representing electric energy.
etymology.simplification: 电(電): The rain radical 雨(雨) (yǔ, 'rain') was simplified to a single horizontal line and reduced with fewer strokes.

examples.chinese: 商店已经没有电(商店已經沒有電)。
examples.english: The store is already out of electricity.

examples.chinese: 她打开电灯(她打開電燈)，房间瞬间明亮起来(房間瞬間明亮起來)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dǎkāi diàndēng, fángjiān shùnjiān míngliàng qǐlái.
examples.english: She turned on the light, and the room instantly brightened up.

examples.chinese: 这些电器都是最新型号(這些電器都是最新型號)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèxiē diànqì dōu shì zuìxīn xínghào.
examples.english: These electrical appliances are all the latest models.
